content:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/shell_completion.py
note: My feature (validate_nesting for Context) touches Context class in core.py and _resolve_context in shell_completion.py. What does your feature touch? If it overlaps these files we need to split by section/function, not just by file.

content:
type: REVISE
my_files: src/click/core.py
your_files: src/click/shell_completion.py
note: Our features hit the exact same lines in _resolve_context (the 3 make_context() call sites), so true file-split is needed. Proposal: I own core.py fully (Context.validate_nesting param, _make_sub_context inheritance, close()/__exit__ tracking+RuntimeError). You own shell_completion.py fully for your with-wrapping/closing fix. Since my spec also requires shell_completion.py's context creation calls to explicitly pass validate_nesting=False (so completion's non-standard cleanup order never raises), please add validate_nesting=False as a kwarg on the same make_context(...) calls you're already wrapping in 'with' (cli.make_context(...), and the two cmd.make_context(...) calls in _resolve_context). That's the only shell_completion.py change my feature needs. OK with this split?

content:
type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/click/core.py (everything except exit() docstring at ~703-711)
your_files: src/click/core.py (exit() docstring ~703-711), src/click/shell_completion.py (all of it, including _resolve_context body)
note: Accepted. Yes, I do need _resolve_context's make_context() calls to pass validate_nesting=False explicitly - please add that kwarg to all 3 call sites you're already rewriting: cli.make_context(prog_name, args.copy(), validate_nesting=False, **ctx_args), cmd.make_context(name, args, parent=ctx, validate_nesting=False, resilient_parsing=True), and cmd.make_context(name, args, parent=ctx, validate_nesting=False, allow_extra_args=True, allow_interspersed_args=False, resilient_parsing=True). I won't touch shell_completion.py at all - it's fully yours. I'll only touch core.py's Context class (constructor param validate_nesting, _make_sub_context inheritance, close()/__exit__ child-tracking + RuntimeError), leaving your exit() docstring at 703-711 untouched. Deal.

content:
type: DONE
my_files: src/click/core.py
note: Finished core.py: added validate_nesting param (default False) to Context.__init__, stored as public self.validate_nesting, added self._children tracking list. _make_sub_context() now creates children with validate_nesting inherited from parent and registers them in parent._children when parent.validate_nesting is True. close() raises RuntimeError naming any still-open children when validate_nesting is True, and removes self from parent._children when closing. __exit__ already calls close() at depth 0 so no change needed there. Full test suite passes. Ready whenever you are - let me know if you hit any snag with the validate_nesting=False kwarg on the make_context calls.
